turns.text: Q3 2025 was another strong quarter. Google Cloud revenues reached $14.9 billion, up 30% year-over-year. Our AI infrastructure — particularly our sixth-generation TPUs — is giving us a structural cost advantage that we expect to sustain. Waymo now operates fully autonomous rides in eight U.S. cities and is expanding to international markets in 2026. We launched Gemini Ultra 2 for enterprise customers in September, already adopted by over 500 large organizations within 30 days.

Total revenues were $103.0 billion, up 15% year-over-year. Operating margin was 35.5%. Google Services revenues grew 12%, with strong performance across Search, YouTube, and Play. Google Cloud operating income was $2.1 billion, with margin expanding meaningfully from prior year. We remain on track for our full-year Cloud revenue target. Free cash flow was $26.3 billion.
Our next question comes from Eric Sheridan at Goldman Sachs.
Sundar, Waymo expansion to eight cities is notable. Can you frame how the unit economics of autonomous ride-hailing get to profitability, and what the timeline looks like?

statements.claim: Google Cloud revenues reached $14.9 billion in Q3 2025, up 30% year-over-year.
Waymo now operates fully autonomous rides in eight U.S. cities and will expand internationally in 2026.
Gemini Ultra 2 was adopted by over 500 large enterprise organizations within 30 days of launch.
Google Cloud operating income was $2.1 billion in Q3 2025 with meaningful margin expansion.
Alphabet's sixth-generation TPUs provide a structural cost advantage expected to be sustained.
